decks.notes: The deck focuses on quickly putting pressure on the opponent with low-cost, efficient creatures and burn spells. It aims to control the board early on with aggressive creatures like Monastery Swiftspear and Dragon's Rage Channeler while using spells like Lightning Bolt and Lava Dart to remove threats and deal direct damage. The deck also includes card draw and selection spells like Preordain and Mishra's Bauble to ensure a steady flow of cards and maintain momentum. Overall, the deck prioritizes speed and aggression to overwhelm the opponent and win the game quickly.

The main concept of this deck is to use a combination of cards to quickly assemble a powerful win condition without relying on traditional lands. By utilizing a variety of spells and creatures, the deck aims to effectively disrupt the opponent's strategy while setting up for a game-ending combo utilizing cards like Goblin Charbelcher, Lotus Bloom, and Thundertrap Trainer. With a focus on both control elements and explosive plays, this deck seeks to outmaneuver opponents and secure victory through clever interactions and strategic decision-making.

This deck focuses on using aggressive creatures to pressure the opponent while disrupting their game plan with cards like Fatal Push and Thoughtseize. The deck also incorporates cards that can flicker creatures like Ephemerate to generate value and protect key pieces. It aims to control the board with creatures like Emperor of Bones and Overlord of the Balemurk, while also applying pressure with cards like Solitude and Orcish Bowmasters. Teferi, Time Raveler provides additional disruption and Witch Enchanter can generate card advantage. The deck's mana base is built to support the colors necessary for the deck's strategy.

The deck focuses on generating powerful combo plays by using cards like Ral, Monsoon Mage and Ruby Medallion in combination with various rituals and card draw spells. The goal is to ramp up mana quickly, generate storm count, and finish off the opponent with a massive Grapeshot. Past in Flames allows for recursion of key spells, while Wish provides flexibility in accessing key sideboard cards. The deck excels at explosive turns and can overwhelm opponents with its quick and efficient combo potential.

This deck focuses on controlling the game through a combination of counterspells, removal, and board wipes. It includes powerful planeswalkers like Teferi, Hero of Dominaria and Teferi, Time Raveler to help generate card advantage and disrupt the opponent's game plan. The deck also utilizes Phlage, Titan of Fire's Fury as a win condition, alongside efficient removal spells like Solitude and Wrath of the Skies. With a mix of control elements and a strong late-game strategy, this deck aims to outlast its opponents and secure victory through incremental advantage.

The main focus of this Boros Aggro deck is to quickly overwhelm opponents with aggressive creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Phlage, Titan of Fire's Fury, while using cards like Goblin Bombardment and Galvanic Discharge to control the board. The deck also includes cards like Ranger-Captain of Eos and Seasoned Pyromancer for added value and card advantage. Additionally, the inclusion of Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Arena of Glory provide additional ways to boost creatures and push for a quick victory. The mana base is built to support the aggressive nature of the deck, with a mix of dual lands and fetch lands to ensure consistency.

The primary concept of this Dimir Control deck is to disrupt your opponent's game plan using counterspells and removal spells while also gaining card advantage through card draw and graveyard recursion. The deck aims to control the board, deny the opponent's resources, and eventually win the game with powerful finishers like Kaito, Bane of Nightmares. The combination of disruption, card advantage, and powerful creatures makes this deck a formidable opponent in the Modern 1 vs 1 format.

The deck revolves around using the Living End combo to cheat creatures from the graveyard into play while controlling the board with counterspells and disruptive creatures. The deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan while setting up its own combo to overwhelm the opponent with powerful creatures. The mix of cascade cards, graveyard manipulation, and disruption gives the deck a versatile and adaptive gameplay style, making it difficult for opponents to predict and counter effectively.

This deck focuses on quickly filling the graveyard with powerful creatures like Griselbrand and Atraxa, then using cards like Goryo's Vengeance to cheat them into play for a big advantage. It also utilizes instant spells like Ephemerate and Prismatic Ending to control the board and protect the combo pieces. The deck aims to win by reanimating big threats early in the game and overwhelming the opponent before they can respond effectively. The strategy is to disrupt the opponent's game plan while setting up a game-winning combo at the same time.

This Boros Aggro deck focuses on aggressive creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Phlage, Titan of Fire's Fury, supported by efficient removal and combat tricks. The deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a fast and powerful board presence, using cards like Goblin Bombardment and Guide of Souls to push for damage. Cards like Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Ranger-Captain of Eos provide additional value and resilience, while also enabling explosive finishes. The deck's mana base is optimized for consistency and speed, allowing it to quickly deploy threats and close out games before the opponent can stabilize.

The deck focuses on generating fast mana with rituals, mana filters, and Ruby Medallion to cast multiple spells in a turn. It uses Ral, Monsoon Mage's ability to copy instants and sorceries, alongside Wrenn's Resolve to draw a large amount of cards. The combo pieces include Glimpse the Impossible to create storm count, Past in Flames to recast spells from the graveyard, and Grapeshot as a win condition. The deck also includes Wish cards for utility and consistency, while striking a balance between card advantage and speed in order to overwhelm the opponent.

The deck focuses on using instant reanimator spells like Goryo's Vengeance to quickly bring back powerful creatures like Griselbrand or Emperor of Bones from the graveyard. This combo strategy is supported by control elements like Thoughtseize and Force of Negation to protect the combo pieces. Additionally, cards like Ephemerate and Faithful Mending provide card advantage and recursion. The deck aims to quickly assemble its combo pieces and overwhelm the opponent with powerful threats, while disrupting their game plan with removal and counter spells.

This deck focuses on utilizing graveyard recursion with cards like Gravecrawler and Marionette Apprentice to generate value with sacrifice effects from cards like Phyrexian Tower and Malevolent Rumble. It also includes a combo finisher with Phoenix Fleet Airship and Warren Soultrader to close out the game quickly. The deck includes disruption with cards like Thoughtseize to control the game and protect the combo pieces. The deck aims to be resilient and efficient in executing its game plan while disrupting the opponent's strategy.

The deck focuses on quickly overwhelming opponents with aggressive creatures and burn spells. It aims to put pressure on the opponent early in the game using efficient creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Guide of Souls, while also having ways to finish off the opponent through direct damage with cards like Galvanic Discharge and Goblin Bombardment. The deck also includes some disruption with Blood Moon and Thraben Charm to disrupt the opponent's game plan. The deck is built to be fast and efficient, aiming to win the game before the opponent can stabilize.

This deck focuses on utilizing artifact creatures and spells to create a fast-paced aggressive strategy. By using cards like Emry, Lurker of the Loch and Salvage Titan, players can quickly deploy threats onto the battlefield while disrupting their opponent's game plan with counter spells like Metallic Rebuke. Cards like Urza's Saga and Mishra's Bauble provide additional value and card advantage, allowing the deck to maintain a strong presence on the board while pressuring the opponent. The deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a combination of efficient creatures and powerful artifacts.

This deck focuses on using repeatable sacrifice effects, such as Malevolent Rumble and Kozilek's Command, in combination with Blade of the Bloodchief to generate a large number of +1/+1 counters on creatures like Glaring Fleshraker and Basking Broodscale. By utilizing Eldrazi Temple and Boseiju, Who Endures, the deck can quickly ramp up into powerful threats like Emrakul, the Promised End and Devourer of Destiny. Urza's Saga and Ugin's Labyrinth provide additional utility and mana generation, while Grove of the Burnwillows offers some lifegain to offset the deck's own damage. The overall strategy is to overwhelm the opponent with a combination of massive creatures and disruptive spells.
